title: Chocolate Cremora Marshmallow Tart for Joanita
excerpt: I'm mad about a cremora tart. So I decided to make the tart to my sister-in-law's taste. It's with a heavy heart that I have to say she passed away before she could taste the tart. seo.title: Chocolate Cremora Marshmallow Tart for Joanita - Marinda Kook

html: I'm mad about a cremora tart.  So I decided to make the tart to my sister-in-law's taste. It's with a heavy heart that I have to say she passed away before she could taste the tart.  I dedicate this tart to my sister-in-law Joanita Engelbrecht.  I would have loved to give her a piece. Life is short. Make the tart and eat it! It's a blend of cremora/subtle chocolate and a marshmallow flavour. It's a big tart made in a springform pan.

recipe.title: Chocolate Cremora Marshmallow Tart for Joanita
recipe.author: Marinda Engelbrecht

items: 2 x boxes Oreo biscuits or any chocolate-based biscuit

steps: Pour the gelatine into the 90 ml water in a cup and set it aside.
Pulse your biscuits until fine in the food processor and mix with the melted butter.

I cut a square piece of baking paper and closed the springform pan over it. In other words, it stuck out well beyond the pan. This just makes it easier to get the tart out of your pan later. Spray the springform pan.
Press the biscuit mixture/crust firmly into the pan. Bring it up a bit against the sides. Put it in the fridge until you need it.
Whisk the Cremora and ice-cold water until all the lumps have disappeared.
Add the condensed milk and whisk for at least 5 minutes. It thickens and starts to look like soft marshmallow.
While whisking, slowly pour in the lemon juice. Then whisk in the vanilla.
Melt the chocolate in the microwave and whisk it in.
Melt the gelatine and water together in the microwave until it dissolves and whisk it in (be careful not to leave it in the microwave too long).
Spoon your Cremora mixture over the biscuits in your pan. Leave it in the fridge overnight.
For the topping: Melt the whole nut chocolate slabs and milk in the microwave. Mix well and pour over your tart. Leave the tart in the fridge overnight.
